observation: Both configurations score 15/15. Graphology is well-documented enough that the model produces correct API usage without a skill. The skill's value is in qualitative improvements: idiomatic addEdge vs addDirectedEdge, explaining the .assign() rendering pattern, and TypeScript generics cleanliness. No assertion failures to drive iteration — skill may benefit from harder evals covering events, custom graph types, or error-prone multi-graph cases.

configurations.qualitative_delta: Uses addDirectedEdge instead of addEdge — redundant on DirectedGraph but not incorrect.
Functionally identical. No explanation of mergeNode vs addNode distinction.
Pre-initializes x/y/community/color at addNode time — those values get overwritten by .assign() calls. Noisy but harmless.
